---
title: "A calibrated whole-cell mechanistic model of human platelet cytosolic calcium signalling"
author: "Steve Haigh"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "STIX Two Text"
    monofont: "Menlo"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    include-in-header: ../pandoc-header.tex
  docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    # reference-doc: thesis-reference.docx  # optional Word style template
bibliography: references.bib
# references.bib is populated on demand from Zotero (verified via the Zotero
# MCP) — no local/hand-made .bib is used. Citations below are written as
# [cite: Author Year] placeholders; replace each with the real [@citekey]
# once that entry is pulled into references.bib.
# Harvard referencing is required by the handbook. Download a Harvard CSL
# (e.g. https://www.zotero.org/styles/harvard-cite-them-right) into this
# folder and uncomment the next line:
# csl: harvard-cite-them-right.csl
---

```{=html}
<!--
================================================================================
DRAFT — MRes Project Dissertation. Structure from Appendix 3 of the MRes
Research Project Handbook; content notes seeded from the feasibility report
reports/design/mike-report-2026-05-14.qmd (PNAS-format draft).

Planning notes are written *[in italic brackets]* — the author's own
convention — and render as visible placeholders. Real drafted prose is left
as prose. Handbook word limits and rules are kept as hidden HTML comments.

WORD-BUDGET NOTE: the feasibility report was a PNAS-style piece with a
different section budget. Three sections need rework to fit the handbook:
  • Methods: feasibility plan was ~1200 words → handbook cap is 800.
  • Results: handbook requires FOUR results figures; the report has one
    data figure (validation) plus a schematic — three mechanistic findings
    are flagged below as candidate figures.
  • Discussion: feasibility plan was ~500 words → handbook wants 1500–2000.
================================================================================
-->
```

## Abstract

```{=html}
<!-- 250–300 words. STRUCTURED using the four bold sub-headings below. -->
```

**Background and Purpose:** *[Platelet activation is initiated by a cytosolic
Ca²⁺ transient, central to haemostasis and to thrombotic disease. Existing
models treat individual Ca²⁺-handling pathways in isolation. Question: can a
calibrated, mass-balanced whole-cell mechanistic model of human platelet
cytosolic Ca²⁺ signalling be built and validated end-to-end?]*

**Experimental Approach:** We adapted the Covert-Lab *wcEcoli* whole-cell
framework [@karr2012; @macklin2020] to platelet biology, integrating
twelve coupled mechanisms — three Gq-coupled receptors (P2Y1, PAR1, PAR4),
the Gαq / PLCβ / PI-cycle cascade, IP3R-mediated dense-tubular-system (DTS)
Ca²⁺ release, SERCA, PMCA, NCX, P2X1, store-operated entry (STIM1 + Orai1),
mitochondrial MCU / NCLX shuttling, and cytosolic and luminal Ca²⁺ buffering
— driven end-to-end by physiological agonist input.

**Key Results:** The model reproduces the Dolan & Diamond (2014) Fig. 4
IP₃-stimulated Ca²⁺ transients, with and without extracellular Ca²⁺, meeting
all acceptance criteria and holding a stable resting state, with no
hand-fitted IP₃ forcing remaining in the calcium pathway. *[Handbook: state
Key Results without quantitative detail — keep the numbers (5/5 criteria,
resting cyt 104 nM, DTS 235 µM) for the Results section.]*

**Conclusion and Implications:** *[First calibrated, mass-balanced
mechanistic platelet whole-cell model; serves as a substrate for downstream
processes (granule release, integrin activation, platelet–CTC interactions);
in principle extensible to cross-disciplinary applications. State the main
limitation briefly.]*

## Graphical abstract

```{=html}
<!--
Single, concise, visual summary — the central finding at a glance.
Simplicity + self-contained. See Elsevier / Springer Nature / NIH links.
-->
```

*[No graphical abstract existed in the feasibility report. Candidate: a
simplified BioRender redraw of the architecture diagram (see @fig-architecture
/ `../figures/model-status-graphviz.png`), reduced to the central
agonist → Ca²⁺-peak message.]*

## Significance statement

```{=html}
<!-- Up to 120 words. Understandable to an undergraduate-educated scientist
     outside their field of specialty. Avoid jargon. -->
```

*[~120 words, aimed at non-specialists. Suggested arc: (1) platelet
activation is a Ca²⁺ event, central to haemostasis and disease; (2) existing
models treat individual pathways in isolation; (3) we built the first
mass-balanced multi-process model with every protein counted and every
reaction calibrated; (4) the model is now a substrate for asking mechanistic
questions that cannot be addressed by isolated-pathway models — e.g. which
extrusion pathway rate-limits cytosolic recovery, how mitochondrial buffering
interacts with DTS refilling.]*

## Introduction

```{=html}
<!-- Up to 600 words. Background & context; critical literature review;
     knowledge gap; aims and hypotheses. -->
```

**The biological question.** Platelet activation is initiated by a cytosolic
Ca²⁺ transient. *[Two to three sentences on the agonist-to-Ca²⁺-peak cascade:
agonist binds Gq-coupled receptors, Gαq activates PLCβ, PIP₂ → IP₃ + DAG,
IP₃ opens IP3R in the DTS membrane (the platelet's ER-equivalent), and DTS
Ca²⁺ floods into the cytosol. Reference [@dolan2014] and the
Mike group's PI-cycle paper [@mazet2020].]*

*[One paragraph on why a whole-cell rather than single-pathway model: resting
state is a multi-component balance; mechanistic predictions require all
components present and mass-balanced; isolated pathway models cannot answer
"what rate-limits recovery" or "how does mitochondrial buffering interact with
DTS refill".]*

**The framework choice.** *[~250 words. Comparison of four options,
motivating wcEcoli.]*

- **COPASI** [@hoops2006] — ODE/stochastic solver, SBML import,
  single-pathway focus. *[Excellent solver, but no native mass-balance across
  compartments, no copy-number accounting, no listener / analysis pipeline.]*
- **Virtual Cell (VCell)** [cite: VCell — catalogue in Zotero] — *[The most
  capable of the dedicated platforms: compartmental ODE *and* spatial
  reaction-diffusion (PDE) modelling, a mature GUI, and SBML / BioModels
  interoperability. Not used because it is still a single-model simulator —
  no whole-cell copy-number accounting, no multi-process mass-balance
  partitioning across concurrent processes, no integrated listener / analysis
  pipeline. Its headline strength, spatial Ca²⁺ microdomains, is not yet
  required by the well-mixed compartmental platelet model, while the
  whole-cell bookkeeping that *is* required falls outside its design centre —
  the same single-network limitation as COPASI, at a higher capability
  tier.]*
- **SBML and BioModels** [@keating2020; @maliksheriff2020] — *[Format and catalogue, not
  engine. Each model in isolation; re-use limited by integration cost.]*
- **wcEcoli** [@macklin2020] — *[The only validated multi-process
  whole-cell model. Mass-balanced; every protein counted; compartmentalised;
  listener / analysis framework. But E. coli-specific biology, ~80 kloc,
  unmaintained since 2022.]*

*[Closing paragraph: we forked wcEcoli, pruned all E. coli biology, and
rebuilt from the platelet literature. Kept the simulation engine, state
partitioning, and analysis framework; replaced reconstruction and processes.
Cost ~1 month of pruning; benefit ~5,000 lines of validated infrastructure we
did not need to write.]*

**Gap this work fills.** *[No prior calibrated multi-process platelet WCM
exists. [@dolan2014] is the closest comparator but treats
Ca²⁺ handling at the fixed-point level with hand-fitted IP₃ forcing.
[@mazet2020] covers the upstream PI-cycle but stops at IP₃. We
connect these with explicit downstream mechanisms and validate end-to-end.]*

## Methods

```{=html}
<!-- Up to 800 words. Approach in enough detail for reproducibility; study
     design, materials, techniques, analysis; justify methods and
     limitations; include any software used.
     NOTE: feasibility plan was ~1200 words — trim to fit 800. -->
```

### Framework adaptation

*[~200 words. Kept: `wholecell/` simulation engine, state classes
(BulkMolecules, UniqueMolecules, LocalEnvironment), listener / analysis
architecture, I/O format. Removed: all `models/ecoli/`,
`reconstruction/ecoli/`, the ParCa pipeline, Cython performance modules.
Added: `models/platelet/`, `reconstruction/platelet/`, `raw_data/` platelet
tables. Replaced ParCa with a directly-constructed `SimulationDataPlatelet`
object.]*

### Biological content — methodology per mechanism

*[~400 words. For each of the 12 mechanisms we followed a fixed five-step
process.]*

1. **Anchor paper selection.** *[Dolan & Diamond 2014 supplied the validation
   experiment (Fig. 4: IP₃-stimulated Ca²⁺ transients with and without
   extracellular Ca²⁺) and resting-state targets (100 nM cyt, 250 µM DTS).]*
2. **Literature review for kinetics.** *[Primary sources per mechanism —
   [cite: De Young & Keizer 1992] / [cite: Li & Rinzel 1994] for
   IP3R gating; [@purvis2008] (with corrections) for SERCA;
   [@caride2007] for PMCA; [@hoover2011]
   / [@dolan2014] for SOCE; [@mazet2020] for the
   PI cycle.]*
3. **Species enumeration.** *[Every Ca²⁺-binding or -gating conformational
   state becomes a tracked species with a compartment tag (e.g. `PMCA[pl]`,
   `SERCA_E1Ca[dts]`). ~50 species across the calcium pathway.]*
4. **Copy-number extraction.** *[[@burkhart2012]
   supplied platelet-specific protein copy numbers; [@coller1995]
   for receptor counts.]*
5. **Rate-constant extraction.** *[Primary sources, units normalised
   (concentrations µM, time s), every value carrying a citation comment in
   code.]*

*[Closing: each mechanism enters the ODE right-hand side as a small set of
fluxes; the integrator (`scipy.integrate.solve_ivp`, BDF) advances species
counts on a 1-second outer timestep.]*

### Sanity-check discipline

**Validation gates.** *[Each increment must pass (i) resting-state stability
over 200 s, (ii) Dolan Fig. 4 IP₃-transient reproduction, (iii) the 21-test
unit suite. Changes that break a gate are reverted.]*

**Calibration-coupled parameters.** *[Some parameters are mutually constrained
— the IP3R single-channel conductance γ\textsubscript{IP3R} is back-derived to
balance SERCA flux at rest; if SERCA constants change it must be re-derived
(an explicit constructor-time assertion).]*

**The Purvis k₃ story.** *[~200-word narrative. (1) After implementing SERCA
from Purvis 2008 Table 1, the resting state failed: cytosolic Ca²⁺ ran away or
collapsed depending on initial conditions. (2) Back-tracing the flux balance
pointed at k₃, governing the E₁P·Ca → E₂P·Ca phosphoryl-transfer step.
(3) Cross-checking Purvis 2008 against its cited primary ([cite: Dode 2002])
revealed the Purvis value was the reciprocal of the Dode
value — a transcription error propagated unchecked. (4) With the corrected
value the resting state held. (5) Three other Purvis values were spot-checked;
one more rounding drift was found.]* *[Methodological upshot: every numerical
value carries a primary-source citation in code, and primaries are consulted
directly when calibration fails — also a warning about AI-assisted parameter
extraction, which confidently reproduces review-paper values without
verifying upstream.]*

### Software and reproducibility

*[~150 words. Python 3.11; public repository `stevehaigh/platelet-wcm`; test
suite; CI (GitHub Actions); Docker images for runtime and webapp; the Dash
webapp at `platelet-wcm.uksouth.azurecontainer.io` for browsing runs.]*

## Results

```{=html}
<!-- Up to 1500 words. FOUR results figures, numbered and referenced in text;
     each embedded immediately below the paragraph that first cites it,
     followed by a numbered legend. Describe WITHOUT interpretation. Error
     bars labelled and described. Correct units.
     NOTE: handbook requires four results figures. Currently one data figure
     (validation) plus a schematic — promote the three mechanistic findings
     below into data figures to reach four. -->
```

### Model architecture

*[~250 words + figure. Walk the diagram top-to-bottom: agonist input, GPCRs in
the plasma membrane, Gαq cascade in the cytosol, PI cycle, IP3R on the DTS
membrane, the Ca²⁺-cyt hub, DTS lumen with multi-protein buffering,
mitochondrial shuttle, plasma-membrane extrusion (PMCA, NCX) and entry
(Orai1, P2X1). No hand-fitted forcing remains in the calcium path.]* As shown
in @fig-architecture:

![Architecture of the platelet Ca²⁺ whole-cell model. Agonist input drives
three Gq-coupled receptors; the Gαq cascade activates PLCβ, which hydrolyses
PIP₂ to IP₃ and DAG; IP₃ opens IP3R channels in the DTS membrane, releasing
stored Ca²⁺; SERCA returns Ca²⁺ to the DTS; PMCA and NCX extrude to the
extracellular space; SOCE refills the cytosol through Orai1; P2X1 provides
ATP-gated fast entry; mitochondria buffer transiently via MCU and NCLX.
Cytosolic and luminal buffering by calmodulin, gelsolin (cyt) and
calreticulin, HSP90B1, BiP, CREC (DTS) is shown explicitly. **[Figure to be
replaced with hand-drawn BioRender version for final
manuscript.]**](../figures/model-status-graphviz.png){#fig-architecture width=100%}

### Validation against Dolan & Diamond 2014

*[~400 words + figure. The headline result. (1) Describe the Dolan Fig. 4
experiment: paired conditions with and without extracellular Ca²⁺, IP₃-driven
transients shaped by presence or absence of SOCE refill. (2) State the five
Phase 3 acceptance criteria (resting cyt 100 ± 10 nM, resting DTS within band,
transient peak height, transient duration, paired-condition difference).
(3) Report 5/5 with resting cyt = 104 nM, DTS = 235 µM, IP₃ = 50 nM,
Gαq-active = 100. (4) Achieved without IP₃ forcing.]* See @fig-validation:

![Free vs. bound Ca²⁺ during a 240-second IP₃-driven transient. Cytosolic
free Ca²⁺ rises from ~100 nM to ~500 nM and recovers within 60 s; DTS free
Ca²⁺ depletes by ~60 % during the peak and refills via SOCE. Cytosolic and
luminal buffer occupancy track the free pools with clear coupling. **[Caption
to be refined: state which condition (with or without extracellular Ca²⁺) is
plotted and add panel labels if dual
condition.]**](../figures/ca-bound-free-v0.4.0.png){#fig-validation width=100%}

### Mechanistic findings

*[~500 words. Two or three findings that emerged from having all components
mechanistically present. Each should become a data figure (see four-figure
note above).]*

1. ***PMCA Vmax rate-limits cytosolic Ca²⁺ recovery at low cytosolic
   concentrations.** Even with mitochondrial buffering active, the late tail
   of recovery is set by PMCA turnover. Testable prediction: pharmacological
   PMCA activation should accelerate recovery; inhibition should prolong Ca²⁺
   tails.]* *[→ candidate Figure 3.]*
2. ***MCU buffering alone does not accelerate DTS recovery** — sequestering
   Ca²⁺ into mitochondria removes it from the cytosol but does not return it
   to the DTS, so DTS refilling remains SOCE-limited. Testable prediction:
   mitochondrial-targeted uptake activators may prolong DTS overshoot rather
   than accelerate return to rest.]* *[→ candidate Figure 4.]*
3. ***The DTS overshoots its resting level during recovery from sustained
   stimulation.** The model predicts a ~1 mM transient in DTS Ca²⁺ during the
   1000–3000 s recovery phase, matching long Ca²⁺ tails in real activated
   platelets. Whether this is biology or model artefact is open.]*

## Discussion

```{=html}
<!-- 1,500–2000 words. Interpret findings critically vs aims and literature;
     strengths, limitations, uncertainties; implications and future
     directions; do not restate results.
     NOTE: feasibility plan was ~500 words — expand substantially. -->
```

*[(1) The mechanistic-end-to-end property — once a calibrated substrate
exists, downstream questions (granule release, integrin activation,
platelet–CTC interactions) attach naturally to it. (2) Limitations —
coarse-grained cytosolic buffering, lumped SOCE machinery, no Ca²⁺
microdomains; the deYoung-Keizer IP3R has a low-Ca²⁺ bistability artefact that
emerges only at sustained stimulation > 2000 s, documented but unresolved.
(3) Open biology question on Purvis SERCA Vmax — the rate constants imply
~5 cycles/s/pump at resting cyt, ~2–5× higher than literature suggests for
SERCA3b at low substrate; resolution awaits new primary-source kinetics.
(4) Methodological generalisation — the per-mechanism workflow (anchor paper
→ literature → species → counts → rates → cross-check) is portable to other
single-cell calibration problems.]*

## Conclusion

```{=html}
<!-- 100–200 words. Summarise the main findings succinctly and reinforce the
     significance of the work. -->
```

*[Seed from the feasibility report's closing paragraph: the model is at
v0.4.1, validated against Dolan & Diamond 2014. Future work splits into
near-term biology (granule release, the P2Y₁₂ inhibitory arm) and longer-term
architecture (a kinetics-as-data refactor to enable contributor
extensibility). Reinforce the significance: first calibrated mass-balanced
mechanistic platelet WCM, now a reusable substrate.]*

## Acknowledgements

```{=html}
<!-- Optional. Not formally assessed. -->
```

*[~50 words. Mike Fry for the upstream PI-cycle framework; the Covert Lab for
the open-source wcEcoli release; any specific advice from collaborators.]*

## AI statement

```{=html}
<!-- See the handbook section "Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
     (GenAI) tools" and complete as required. -->
```

*[Complete per the handbook's GenAI section. Note: the Purvis k₃ episode in
Methods is relevant context for an honest account of where AI assistance was
and was not relied upon for parameter extraction.]*

## References

```{=html}
<!-- Harvard style; majority journal articles. Generated by Quarto from
     references.bib, which is populated on demand from Zotero (verified via
     the Zotero MCP). References do not count towards the word limit. -->
```

::: {#refs}
:::

## Appendices

```{=html}
<!-- If needed: supplementary data, extended tables, raw data, detailed
     protocols. -->
```

*[Candidates: the Purvis-vs-Dode k₃ correction table; the full species and
rate-constant tables with per-value primary citations; the 21-test validation
suite summary.]*
